title: Sampling and Extraction of Lyophilized Plant Tissue for LC-MS Analysis
author: Kateřina Kučerová
materials: Preparation of Sampling Materials and Equipment 50 mL Falcon tubes, a scalpel or knife, scissors, 96 % ethanol for surface sterilization, disposable gloves for handling, a transport box containing dry ice.
Preparation of Materials and Equipment for Extraction EtOH-H 2 O (75:25) CH 3 CN-H 2 O (50:50) 2.0 mL Eppendorf tubes Stainless steel grinding beads Pipettes with tips (100–1000 μL] Labels.

steps.title: Cut a defined portion of fresh plant tissue (e
steps.instruction: Cut a defined portion of fresh plant tissue (e.g., leaf, root, stem, flower, or fruit) and immediately transfer it into a sterile 50 mL conical centrifuge tube.

steps.title: Immediately place the tubes on dry...
steps.instruction: Immediately place the tubes on dry ice to preserve sample integrity during transport to the laboratory.

steps.title: Cover the opening of the tube...
steps.instruction: Cover the opening of the tube with a single piece of fine mesh fabric to allow airflow while preventing contamination.

steps.title: Secure the mesh fabric over the...
steps.instruction: Secure the mesh fabric over the tube opening using a rubber band to ensure it remains firmly in place.

steps.title: Place the prepared tubes into the...
steps.instruction: Place the prepared tubes into the lyophilizer flasks, ensuring a stable and upright position to facilitate efficient drying.

steps.title: Perform lyophilization for a duration of...
steps.instruction: Perform lyophilization for a duration of 3 days, following the manufacturer’s operating guidelines for optimal drying efficiency.

steps.title: Transfer the lyophilized plant material into...
steps.instruction: Transfer the lyophilized plant material into an analytical mill and grind to a fine, homogeneous powder.

steps.title: Store the ground plant material in...
steps.instruction: Store the ground plant material in a tightly sealed Falcon tube under dry room conditions to prevent moisture absorption.

steps.title: Weigh 25 ± 2
steps.instruction: Weigh 25 ± 2.5 mg of lyophilized plant material into a 2.0 mL round-bottom Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Add one stainless steel grinding bead...
steps.instruction: Add one stainless steel grinding bead to each tube to facilitate homogenization.

steps.title: Homogenize the samples using a TissueLyser...
steps.instruction: Homogenize the samples using a TissueLyser at 25 rpm for 30 seconds .

steps.title: Add 2 000 μL of EtOH-H...
steps.instruction: Add 2 000 μL of EtOH-H 2 O solution (75:25, v/v) to each tube and vortex briefly to ensure thorough mixing.

steps.title: Incubate the samples at 40 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the samples at 40 °C for 3 minutes to enhance extraction efficiency.

steps.title: Homogenize the samples using a TissueLyser...
steps.instruction: Homogenize the samples using a TissueLyser at 25 rpm for 60 seconds .

steps.title: Centrifuge the samples at 14 000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the samples at 14 000 rpm for 5 minutes to pellet debris.

steps.title: Carefully transfer 500 μL of the...
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer 500 μL of the supernatant into a new 2.0 mL Eppendorf tube, avoiding any particulate matter.

steps.title: Evaporate the solvent by drying the...
steps.instruction: Evaporate the solvent by drying the samples in a SpeedVac concentrator until completely dry.

steps.title: Resuspend the dried extract in 2...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the dried extract in 2 000 μL of CH 3 CN-H 2 O solution (50:50, v/v)

steps.title: Sonicate the samples for 15 seconds...
steps.instruction: Sonicate the samples for 15 seconds , followed by vortexing for 30 seconds to ensure complete dissolution.

steps.title: Centrifuge the samples at 14 000...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the samples at 14 000 rpm for 5 minutes to clarify the extract.

steps.title: Carefully transfer the clarified supernatant into...
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the clarified supernatant into LC-MS vials, avoiding any residual particulates.
